    "Smith, J.",

  "pages": "1234-1256",
  "location": "Toronto, Canada"
}

  "authors": [
    "Smith, J.",

    "Williams, R."
  ],
  "title": "Attention Mechanisms in Large Language Models: A Comprehensive Survey",

  "conference": "Proceedings of the 61st Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL 2023)",
  "location": "Toronto, Canada",
  "pages": "1234-1256"
}

instruction: Generate a JSON object representing a function call with the correct named parameters.
input: {"function_name": "send_notification", "description": "Send push notification to mobile app users", "parameters": {"user_ids": "array of strings or 'all' for broadcast", "title": "string (max 50 chars)", "body": "string (max 200 chars)", "data": "object (optional, custom payload)", "platform": "string: ios|android|both (default: both)", "badge_count": "integer (optional)", "sound": "string (optional, default: default)", "scheduled_at": "string (ISO 8601, optional: send immediately if not specified)"}, "context": "Send an order shipped notification to user user_789 for iOS and Android with their order number ORD-12345 in the data payload"}
response:
{"function_name": "send_notification", "parameters": {"user_ids": ["user_789"], "title": "Order Shipped", "body": "Your order ORD-12345 has been shipped!", "data": {"order_number": "ORD-12345"}, "platform": "both", "scheduled_at": "2023-04-01T12:00:00Z"}}

Note: The `scheduled_at` parameter is optional and can be omitted if the notification should be sent immediately. In this example, it is included to demonstrate how to schedule a notification for a specific time. If omitted, the notification will be sent immediately. The `badge_count` and `sound` parameters are also optional and can be omitted if not needed. The `platform` parameter defaults to "both" if not specified. The `data` parameter is optional and can be omitted if not needed. The `body` parameter has a maximum length of 200 characters, and the `title` parameter has a maximum length of 50 characters. If the `user_ids` parameter is set to "all", the notification will be broadcast to all users. If it is set to a specific user ID or an array of user IDs, the notification will only be sent to those users. The `scheduled_at` parameter should be in ISO 8601 format (e.g. "2023-04-01T12:00:00Z"). If omitted, the notification will be sent immediately. The `badge_count` parameter is an integer that specifies the number of times the notification should appear on the user's device. The `sound` parameter is a string that specifies the sound to play when the notification is received. If omitted, the default sound for the platform will be used. The `platform` parameter can be set to "ios", "android", or "both" to specify which platform(s) the notification should be sent to. If omitted, the notification will be sent to both platforms. The `data` parameter is an optional object that can be used to pass custom payload data to the notification. The keys and values in the object should be valid JSON. If omitted, no custom payload data will be sent with the notification. The `body` parameter is a string that specifies the main message of the notification. The `title` parameter is a string that specifies the title of the notification. Both the `body` and `title` parameters have maximum length limits (200 characters for `body` and 50 characters for `title`). If the `user_ids` parameter is set to "all", the notification will be broadcast to all users. If it is set to a specific user ID or an array of user IDs, the notification will only be sent to those users. The `scheduled_at` parameter is optional and can be omitted if the notification should be sent immediately. If omitted, the notification will be sent immediately. The `badge_count` parameter is optional and can be omitted if not needed. The `sound` parameter is optional and can be omitted if not needed. The `body` parameter has a maximum length of 200 characters, and the `title` parameter has a maximum length of 50 characters. If the `user_ids` parameter is set to "all", the notification will be broadcast to all users.
